title: salix lasiandra var. lasiandra
intro: Salix lasiandra var. lasiandra, also known as Pacific willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Pacific coast of North America. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of Pacific willow have been found to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting digestive health. Pacific willow can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various ailments.
